req.title: Standing order (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-095 defines two orthogonal behaviours: (1) temporal validation of start_date and (2) idempotency via Idempotency-Key. EP partitions dates into {future, today, past}. BVA identifies today as the exact boundary. STC covers the idempotency state machine: first call transitions the resource from non-existent to scheduled; second identical call must stay in scheduled and return the cached 201. DT captures the combined (date-validity x idempotency-key) decision space. NEG covers the explicit error pathway. Together these five tests achieve full coverage of the stated acceptance criteria.

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_future_date_returns_201
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and a valid bearer token is available via <auth_fixture>
start_date is at least today + 1 day (clearly in the future partition)
actions: Build POST /standing-orders payload with amount, currency, frequency, and start_date = today + 7 days
trace: R-FIN-095: future start dates shall be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_create_standing_order_future_date_returns_201(api_client, auth_headers):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": "100.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert body["start_date"] == start_date

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_past_date_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and a valid bearer token is available via <auth_fixture>
start_date is set to yesterday (clearly in the past partition)
actions: Build POST /standing-orders payload with start_date = today - 1 day
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing start_date
No standing order resource is created
trace: R-FIN-095: past start dates shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_create_standing_order_past_date_returns_422(api_client, auth_headers):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": "100.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_today_date_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and a valid bearer token is available via <auth_fixture>
start_date is exactly today (boundary value: start_date == today)
actions: Build POST /standing-orders payload with start_date = today (date.today())
expected: Confirms that the boundary 'today' is treated as invalid (start_date <= today rule)
trace: R-FIN-095: error pathway — start_date <= today includes the boundary today
BVA: lower boundary of the valid future-date partition

    payload = {
        "amount": "100.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()

tests.name: test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and a valid bearer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A fixed Idempotency-Key is chosen for both calls
actions: Send first POST /standing-orders with a future start_date and a fixed Idempotency-Key
Capture the first response (status code, body)
Send an identical second POST /standing-orders with the same Idempotency-Key
expected: First call returns HTTP 201 with status 'scheduled'
Second call returns HTTP 201 (or 200) with identical 'id' and 'status'
No duplicate resource is created (same id on both responses)
trace: R-FIN-095: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
STC: state transitions non-existent -> scheduled (first call) -> scheduled (second call, no state change)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(api_client, auth_headers):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": "50.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp1 = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    body1 = resp1.json()
    resp2 = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body1["id"] == body2["id"]
    assert body2["status"] == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_decision_table_date_validity_vs_idempotency_key_new_key_past_date
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and a valid bearer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A fresh Idempotency-Key is generated (no prior call with this key)
actions: Build POST /standing-orders payload with start_date = today - 30 days
Send the request — decision row: {new key, past date} -> must reject
expected: HTTP 422 regardless of whether the Idempotency-Key is new
Response body indicates start_date validation failure
No standing order is persisted (idempotency cache must NOT cache error responses)
trace: R-FIN-095: date validation takes precedence; new Idempotency-Key does not bypass it
DT: row — valid-key=new, date=past => 422, no resource created
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_decision_table_date_validity_vs_idempotency_key_new_key_past_date(
    api_client, auth_headers
):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": "200.00",
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text or "date" in error_text
